name: propelauth-flask

metadata.name: propelauth-flask
metadata.summary: A library for managing authentication in Flask

documentation:
# PropelAuth Flask SDK

A Flask library for managing authentication, backed by [PropelAuth](https://www.propelauth.com/?utm_campaign=github-flask). [PropelAuth](https://www.propelauth.com?ref=github) makes it easy to add authentication and authorization to your B2B/multi-tenant application. Your frontend gets a beautiful, safe, and customizable login screen. Your backend gets easy authorization with just a few lines of code. You get an easy-to-use dashboard to config and manage everything. ## Documentation - Full reference this library is [here](https://docs.propelauth.com/reference/backend-apis/flask) - Getting started guides for PropelAuth are [here](https://docs.propelauth.com/) ## Questions? Feel free to reach out at support@propelauth.com

methods.name: propelauth_flask.user.LoggedInUser.is_impersonated
documentation: Returns true if the user is impersonated

methods.name: propelauth_flask.user.LoggedInUser.get_active_org
documentation: Returns the active org member info, if the user has an active org.

methods.name: propelauth_flask.user.LoggedInUser.get_active_org_id
documentation: Returns the active org id, if the user has an active org.

methods.name: propelauth_flask.user.LoggedInUser.get_org
documentation: Returns the org member info for the org_id, if the user is in the org.

methods.name: propelauth_flask.user.LoggedInUser.get_org_by_name
documentation: Returns the org member info for the org_name, if the user is in the org.

methods.name: propelauth_flask.user.LoggedInUser.get_user_property
documentation: Returns the user property value, if it exists.

methods.name: propelauth_flask.user.LoggedInUser.get_orgs
documentation: Returns the orgs the user is in.

methods.name: propelauth_flask.user.LoggedInUser.is_role_in_org
documentation: Returns true if the user is the role in the org.

methods.name: propelauth_flask.user.LoggedInUser.is_at_least_role_in_org
documentation: Returns true if the user is at least the role in the org.

methods.name: propelauth_flask.user.LoggedInUser.has_permission_in_org
documentation: Returns true if the user has the permission in the org.

methods.name: propelauth_flask.user.LoggedInUser.has_all_permissions_in_org
documentation: Returns true if the user has all the permissions in the org.

classes.name: propelauth_flask.user.LoggedOutUser
documentation: If a user is not logged in, optional_user will still allow the request to continue
Ideally, current_user would just be none. However, since current_user is a proxy,
the check `current_user is None` actually returns false.
You can do current_user._get_current_object() is None but that feels clunky.
Instead, we'll make it an explicit type add a function `exists()` to distinguish the cases.

functions.name: propelauth_flask.init_auth
documentation: Fetches metadata required to validate access tokens and returns auth decorators and utilities

variables.name: propelauth_flask.current_user
documentation: Returns the current user. Must be used with one of require_user, optional_user, or require_org_member

variables.name: propelauth_flask.current_org
documentation: Returns the current org. Must be used with require_org_member
